name: zsazsa-pir
description: Priority Intelligence Requirement (PIR) managed by the zsazsa CTI program

question.description: The intelligence question this PIR aims to answer

context.description: Why this question is being asked and what triggered the request

priority.description: MoSCoW priority (Must have / Should have / Could have / Won't have)

time-sensitivity.description: How urgently the PIR must be answered

owner-uuid.description: UUID of the owning stakeholder MISP event

deadline.description: Hard deadline for answering this PIR (ISO 8601)

sub-questions.description: JSON array of sub-questions that make up this PIR

decision-supported.description: The decision this PIR is intended to support

decision-maker.description: Person or role responsible for the decision

consequence.description: Risk or consequence if the PIR is not answered

geographic-scope.description: JSON array of countries/regions in scope (from MISP Country galaxy)

time-frame.description: Time period in scope for collection (free text)

threat-types.description: JSON array of threat type strings in scope (free text, e.g. Ransomware, APT)

threat-actors.description: JSON array of threat actor names in scope (from MISP Threat Actor galaxy)

sectors.description: JSON array of industry sectors in scope (from MISP Sector galaxy)

out-of-scope.description: JSON array of explicitly excluded topics or areas (free text)

technology.description: JSON array of technology scope values

vendor.description: JSON array of vendor scope values

incident.description: JSON array of related incident identifiers

campaign.description: JSON array of related campaign names

collection-sources.description: JSON array of collection source names

output-format.description: JSON array of expected deliverable formats (Flash report, Threat brief, etc.)

distribution.description: JSON array of stakeholder UUIDs for the deliverable recipients (legacy records may still contain stakeholder names)

resolution-note.description: Summary of how the PIR was answered when closing or retiring

next-review.description: Date of the next scheduled review (ISO 8601)

intake-status.description: Intake workflow state: submitted, acknowledged, triaged, approved, rejected, deferred, merged

triaged-at.description: Date the PIR triage was completed (ISO 8601)

decision-at.description: Date the intake decision was recorded (ISO 8601)

rejection-reason.description: Reason for rejecting the PIR at intake

deferral-reason.description: Reason for deferring the PIR at intake

linked-pir-uuid.description: UUID of the PIR this one was merged into

mitre-attack-techniques.description: JSON array of MITRE ATT\&CK technique strings (from misp-galaxy:mitre-attack-pattern)

triage-checklist.description: JSON array of completed triage checklist items

acknowledged-by.description: User who acknowledged the PIR at intake

triaged-by.description: User who completed the PIR triage

decision-by.description: User who recorded the intake decision

creator.description: User who created this PIR (set at creation, not editable)
